summary: electron-orbit harvests installer env vars on postinstall and exfiltrates via hand-rolled TLS client on import
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall script (`install.js`, configured via package.json `"postinstall": "node install.js"`) writes a marker file to `os.tmpdir()/electron_orbit_install_marker.txt` containing `hostname`, `username`, `cwd`, and a JSON dump of `process.env` filtered only to drop keys containing the substring 'path'. This sweeps installer secrets such as `AWS_*`, `GITHUB_TOKEN`, `NPM_TOKEN`, and CI provider tokens. The reads of `process` and `env` are concealed via an XOR helper (`__s(key, data)`) that decodes to `globalThis['process']['env']`, hiding the access from static scanners. On `require()`/`import` of the package's main entry, top-level code reads back the staged marker file, XOR-transforms its contents with the key `electron-orbit`, and invokes a hand-rolled TLS 1.3 client implemented in `src/aetherls.ts` (raw `net.connect(443, host)` plus custom ClientHello/ECDHE/AES-GCM/HKDF) to send an HTTP GET (`User-Agent: aetherget/0.1`) carrying the transformed payload. The destination host is gated by a sha256-preimage check on `process.env.BuildType` matching the hardcoded digest `0ceaa396e2134a9937563e841539f30a9995e6e8646e60dcec24b26dc5748295`, a staged-rollout / anti-analysis kill switch. The package name and README invoke the 'Electron' brand but the code contains no Electron integration; the postinstall additionally writes a JSON blob with fabricated ELF/Mach-O/MZ magic bytes as a decoy 'native bootstrap'. The combination of bulk env harvest, XOR obfuscation of network primitives, env-var-gated activation, a custom TLS stack designed to evade standard network-monitoring hooks, and brand-impersonation framing is an unambiguous install-then-beacon supply-chain attack.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
